filesystem.description: File system access for reading/writing files

github.description: GitHub integration for issues, PRs, and repository operations

slack.description: Slack integration for messaging and channel operations

memory.description: Persistent memory for storing and retrieving information

_comments.usage: Copy this file to your project root and configure the servers you need
_comments.security: Never commit this file with actual tokens - use environment variables
_comments.customization: Remove unused servers and update paths for your project
_comments.governance: MCP enablement is constrained at the user tier: global/settings.json sets enableAllProjectMcpServers:false with an empty enabledMcpjsonServers allowlist, so a project-supplied .mcp.json is never auto-enabled - each server must be approved explicitly. Consistent with the repo's secret-blocking posture (#677).
_comments.successor-migration: Intentionally deferred (#677): this template keeps the @modelcontextprotocol namespace rather than migrating github/postgres/slack to third-party successors, to avoid endorsing third parties. The successor pointers in 'servers' below are informational only.
_comments.servers: Reference servers live under the @modelcontextprotocol namespace (NOT @anthropic). filesystem and memory are actively maintained. github, postgres, and slack reference servers are deprecated upstream; the maintained successors are GitHub's official HTTP server (https://api.githubcopilot.com/mcp/ - see .mcp.json.example for the http transport shape), @bytebase/dbhub for PostgreSQL, and the community slack-mcp-server. Verify the current package before relying on it in production.
